meta.siteTitle: Wadi El Nile Derma — Therapeutic Moisturizers
meta.brandSub: Faculty of Pharmacy — Wadi El Nile University
meta.siteDescription: Student-developed therapeutic moisturizers from the Faculty of Pharmacy, Wadi El Nile University.

home.heroBadge: Faculty of Pharmacy · Wadi El Nile University
home.heroTitle: Science-led hydration for every skin type
home.heroSubtitle: A series of therapeutic moisturizers created by pharmacy students—lightweight for oily skin, rich for dry skin, and balanced for combination skin.
home.overviewLead: Healthy skin starts with the right barrier support. Our three formulas target distinct dermatological needs while sharing a commitment to clarity, safety, and evidence-informed ingredient choices.
home.overviewP1: Each variant is built around emollients and humectants selected for how they behave on the skin—not just on the label. The oily-skin formula prioritizes non-greasy hydration; the dry-skin formula emphasizes occlusion and lasting comfort; the combination formula focuses on zone-to-zone balance.
home.overviewP2: This educational initiative reflects how future pharmacists think about formulation: stability, patient counseling, and realistic expectations for cosmetic–therapeutic skincare.
home.productsSectionLead: Choose the line that matches your skin’s primary pattern. Detailed ingredient stories and mechanisms are on each product page.
home.cardOilyDesc: Water-based hydration with glycerin and light oils to moisturize without a heavy film.
home.cardDryDesc: Beeswax and rich emollients help lock in moisture and soften tight, flaky areas.
home.cardComboDesc: Jojoba and shea support varied zones while glycerin and vitamin E reinforce protection.
home.trust1Text: Each active is explained with its cosmetic role so users and peers can learn, not guess.
home.trust2Text: Developed under pharmacy training with attention to compatibility and skin physiology.
home.trust3Text: A clean, international-standard layout suitable for portfolios, posters, and stakeholder review.
home.ctaBandTitle: Ready to dive into the science?
home.ctaBandText: Open a product page for full ingredient breakdowns, properties, and expected skin effects.

footer.tagline: Wadi El Nile University — Faculty of Pharmacy
footer.note: Educational project. Not a substitute for medical advice.

oily.intro: A lighter therapeutic moisturizer designed for sebum-rich skin: humectants draw water into the stratum corneum while almond oil and shea provide emollience in a controlled, spreadable base.
oily.prop1: Aqueous base (Aqua) for fast spreadability and refreshing skin feel.
oily.prop2: Glycerin as a classic humectant to improve short-term hydration and suppleness.
oily.prop3: Sweet almond oil and shea butter as emollients that soften without an overly occlusive finish when used in balanced ratios.
oily.eff1: Supports a more comfortable, less tight sensation after cleansing.
oily.eff2: Helps reduce the look of superficial dryness that can accompany oilier skin types.
oily.eff3: Contributes to a smoother, more even skin surface for daily wear under sunscreen or makeup.
oily.aquaRole: Solvent and base; enables even distribution of humectants and lipids across the skin.
oily.glycerinRole: Humectant; binds water in the outer skin layers to improve flexibility and comfort.
oily.almondRole: Emollient plant oil; helps soften and smooth the skin’s surface texture.
oily.sheaRole: Rich emollient; supports barrier lipids and long-lasting skin conditioning.

dry.intro: A richer orientation for low-sebum or dehydrated skin: beeswax adds occlusive character, while almond oil, vitamin E, and shea butter work together to soften, protect, and reduce transepidermal water loss.
dry.prop1: Beeswax contributes a protective, film-forming quality that helps seal in moisture.
dry.prop2: Sweet almond oil delivers emollient fatty acids for suppleness and glide.
dry.prop3: Vitamin E (tocopherol) is widely used as an antioxidant in topical products to support formula integrity and skin-facing benefits.
dry.eff1: Intended to relieve feelings of roughness and tightness associated with dryness.
dry.eff2: Helps the skin look more plumped and luminous when hydration is improved.
dry.eff3: Supports overnight or cold-weather routines where heavier protection is desired.
dry.beeswaxRole: Natural occlusive and thickener; slows water loss and increases product body.
dry.almondRole: Nourishing emollient; improves spread and skin softness.
dry.vitaminERole: Antioxidant; helps protect lipids in the formula and on the skin from oxidative stress.
dry.sheaRole: Intensive emollient; reinforces comfort and barrier-related care.

combination.intro: Formulated for skin that behaves differently across the face: jojoba oil is often chosen for a skin-similar feel, glycerin hydrates, vitamin E supports antioxidant care, and shea butter conditions without forcing a one-note heavy finish.
combination.prop1: Jojoba oil: liquid wax esters with a light, elegant skin feel popular in combination routines.
combination.prop2: Glycerin: reliable humectant for water retention in drier zones.
combination.prop3: Vitamin E and shea: complementary emollient–antioxidant pairing for daily protection and comfort.
combination.eff1: Aims to hydrate drier patches while remaining appropriate for oilier areas when applied in a thin layer.
combination.eff2: Supports a more unified skin texture between zones over time with consistent use.
combination.eff3: Works well as a versatile day cream when paired with sun protection.
combination.jojobaRole: Emollient with esters resembling human sebum; prized for balance and elegant after-feel.
combination.glycerinRole: Humectant; boosts hydration where the barrier needs water-binding support.
combination.vitaminERole: Antioxidant tocopherol; stabilizing and skin-conditioning connotations in cosmetic science.
combination.sheaRole: Conditioning butter; helps smooth and comfort variable skin areas.
